statement: A larger deep-search pass is unlikely to materially change the immediate implementation plan; the next highest-value step is local patching.
rationale: Official Convex docs reinforce smaller indexed mutations and explicit scheduling patterns, but they do not suggest a different fix order than the local evidence already implies.
keyAssumptions: No hidden external platform behavior is responsible for these failures beyond known Convex limits and retry semantics.
validationCriteria: The first implementation iteration centers on local code changes rather than further research.
If the first patch set fails, additional deep research can be revisited as a second-order step.
critiquePoints: Line-level profiling of the timeout-heavy mutations is still missing and may be needed after the semantic fix.
questions: Should a dedicated follow-up blueprint focus solely on reconcile-path profiling if timeouts persist after terminalization?
